Table 4: Ablation results. We incrementally add each component in STAMP on training baseline. For AndroidWorldM and MemGUI-Bench, we report the average task accuracy over L1–L3 under pass@1 and pass@3.
Table 3: Main results on Memory-World. We report memory accuracy (M-Acc) and task accuracy (T-Acc) under pass@1 and pass@3.
Table 19: Prompt used by the task generation agent.
Table 9: Example of a platform seed used for virtual environment generation.

Figure 2: The end-to-end framework of STAMP. The proposed pipeline consists of three sequential phases: Phase I (Left): Seed configuration guided automated virtual environment synthesis and dynamic expert trajectory collection. Phase II (Middle): Successful trajectory post-processing, memory data isolation, and step-level CoT target annotation. Phase III (Right): Model training via offline step-balanced supervised fine-tuning and online sandbox reinforcement learning.
